key_insight: The paper extends Perron--Frobenius theory and eigenvector centrality from nonnegative real networks to networks with complex edge weights by defining generalized PF conditions and centralities.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers and scientists a principled way to rank nodes and analyze influence in complex-valued systems arising in quantum, electrical, chemical, and communication models.
method: Generalize PF-type theorems to complex-valued matrices
Define eigenvector-based centrality measures for complex-weighted networks
Prove existence results and work through illustrative application examples
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical proofs plus computed centralities on several example networks drawn from electron transport, circuit analysis, mathematical chemistry, and communication systems.
watch_item: Complex edge weights can make eigenvectors harder to interpret, so the chosen generalized PF assumptions and normalization must be checked carefully for each application.
paper_title: Generalizing Perron--Frobenius theory and eigenvector-based centralities to networks with complex edge weights
